sections.paragraph:
πολιά, ᾶς, ἡ (fem. subst. of the adj. [in use since Hom.] πολιός, ά, όν ‘gray’) old age (Cornutus 23 p. 44, 19; Lucian, Philops. 23; Ps.-Lucian, Amor. 12; Themist. p. 163d; 182b; LXX; Philo, Spec. Leg. 2, 238) ἀγαθὴ πολ. (cp. Judg 8:32 A πολιὰ ἀγαθή) MPol 13:2 v.l. (for πολιτεία).—DELG s.v. πολιός.

fem. fem. = feminine

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

adj. adj. = adjective

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Cornutus Cornutus , I a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

Themist Themist , IV a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 844
